chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَوْمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله )

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/253)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/253)
text: كُنَّ نِسَاءُ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِذَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِنَّ صِيَامٌ أَخَّرْنَ ذَلِكَ إِلَى شَعْبَانَ كَرَاهَةَ أَنْ يَمْنَعْنَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَإِذَا كَانَ شَعْبَانُ صُمْنَ وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَقُولُ شَعْبَانُ شَهْرِي .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The wives of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, whenever they had compensatory Fasts upon them, would delay that to Shaban in anticipation that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> would forbid them. So whenever it was Shaban, they would Fast these, and Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> was saying: ‘Shaban is my<sup>saww</sup> month’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that wives of the Holy Prophet, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, when required to fast, would wait up to the month of Sha‘ban, due to anticipation that he may forbid them. In the month of Sha‘ban they would fast and the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘Sha‘ban is my month.’”
